---
title: ""
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

```{=html}
<div class="hero-band">
  <div class="container">
    <div class="row align-items-left g-5">
      <div class="col-md-auto text-center">
      <img src="images/Headshot.png" class="profile-photo" alt="Dr. Andrew E. Anderson">
      </div>
      <div class="col">
        <h1 style="font-size:2.6rem; color:#3c5c3e; line-height:1.15; margin-bottom:0.2rem;">
          Dr. Andrew E. Anderson
        </h1>
        <p style="font-family:Arial,sans-serif; font-size:1rem; color:#6e7d8a; margin-bottom:1.1rem;">
          Assistant Professor &amp; Extension Specialist in Livestock Economics · Utah State University
        </p>
        <p style="max-width:560px;">
          I study the economics of livestock production, with a focus on beef cattle markets,
          rangeland management, and risk management tools for cow-calf and
          stocker operators. My extension program delivers practical economic analysis directly to
          producers and industry stakeholders across the region.
        </p>
        <div class="mb-3">
          <span class="tag">Production Economics</span>
          <span class="tag">Agricultural Prices</span>
          <span class="tag">Rangeland Economics</span>
          <span class="tag">Livestock Risk Management</span>
        </div>
        <div style="display:flex; gap:12px; flex-wrap:wrap; margin-top:4px;">
          <a href="work.qmd" class="btn-sage">Research &amp; Extension</a>
          <a href="cv.pdf" class="btn-outline">Download CV</a>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

## About

<span class="section-label">Background</span>
<div class="rule-accent"></div>

I am an assistant professor and extension economist in the Department of Agricultural & Resource Economics at Utah State University. My work centers on the economics of livestock production and marketing across the Intermountain West.

I completed my Ph.D. at Kansas State University, where my dissertation examined market impacts of the PRF Rainfall Index Insurance program and loss aversion among meat consumers. I was raised in rural Idaho, surrounded by agriculture, and that practical background continues to inform my everyday work.

::: {.row .mt-4}
::: {.col-md-8}

### Research & Extension Focus

- Livestock market price dynamics and basis
- Livestock production decisions 
- Livestock risk management
- Economics of rangeland improvements


:::
:::

### Contact

**Email:** [a.anderson@usu.edu](mailto:a.anderson@usu.edu)
**Office:** AGRS 227

[Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=sE1rhdYAAAAJ&hl=en) · [ORCID](https://orcid.org/0009-0000-9478-0703)

:::
:::
